seed: trust vs suspicion; signal_vs_noise; split_vertical; diagonal_fall; stairs against ash_field; pressure_border; soft_glow_vs_hard_shadow; line; resilience_fragility | abstract variation request 176; target batch count 500
title: Between Echoes
reasoning: This artwork translates the emotional tension between trust and suspicion through a color palette that juxtaposes warm and cool tones. The main colors, a soft powder pink and stark carbon black, represent the duality of warmth (trust) and severity (suspicion). The layout creates a pressure corner where the dark colors dominate, creating a sense of weight and distance while the light colors attempt to break through, suggesting resilience against fragmentation. The arrangement of colors flows from the corner to the center, symbolizing how trust can emerge from the depths of suspicion; irregular patterns of glitch and tide are used to illustrate the chaotic signals of noise. A few broken pixels add intentional disruption, reflecting emotional scars from the past, tying back to the seed's themes of resilience and fragility.

layout.name: pressure_corner
layout.description: One corner is overfilled and the rest of the square reacts with spreading marks.

composition.name: signal_vs_noise
composition.idea: One readable pulse tries to survive inside static, interference, and false echoes.

referenceExample.title: Tiny Hope Under Pressure
referenceExample.pattern: dark frame with single bright center pixel

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
